codeReview.summary: Created comprehensive tests for both Navbar components (MainNavbar for public pages and authenticated Navbar). The implementation includes 20 tests total: 11 for MainNavbar and 9 for the authenticated Navbar. All tests pass successfully. The feature fulfills the validation contract VAL-COMP-002.

issues.description: Test 'renders the notification button' is weak - it only verifies that buttons exist (using getAllByTestId), not specifically a notification button. However, the component does have a notification BellIcon button, so this could be made more specific.
The usePage mock returns static data. While this is sufficient for current tests, it could be made more flexible with factory functions if more complex user state testing is needed in the future.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature specifies skillName 'worker' but there is no worker skill defined in .factory/skills directory. The worker appears to be a general-purpose agent that follows implicit procedures rather than a documented skill.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json shows skillName: 'worker' but .factory/skills/ contains only ralph-tui-* skills

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md documents test setup but doesn't explicitly document the mocking pattern used for UI components (Avatar, Button, DropdownMenu, etc.). The worker created comprehensive mocks for all external dependencies following standard React testing practices.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The test file mocks 9 different component modules. AGENTS.md only mentions general mocking guidance ('Mock external dependencies (axios, localStorage)')

summary: The feature successfully implements comprehensive tests for the Navbar components. The tests cover rendering of both MainNavbar (public) and authenticated Navbar, including navigation links, logo, theme toggle, mobile menu, user avatar, and accessibility roles. All 20 tests pass. The implementation follows project conventions and uses @testing-library/react appropriately. No blocking issues found.
